items.title: Rescuers search rubble for survivors as Venezuela earthquakes kill at least 235
items.summary: At least 4,300 people have been injured after two seven-plus magnitude shallow quakes hit seconds apart.

items.title: Earthquake is devastating blow to Venezuela at time of uncertainty
items.summary: The twin quakes come less than six months after Venezuela's then-leader Nicolás Maduro was seized by US forces.

items.title: People flee as building collapses on Caracas outskirts
items.summary: The capital and its surrounding areas have seen some of the worst damage from the 7.2 and 7.5 quakes, which hit a minute apart.

items.title: UN pauses Strait of Hormuz evacuation plan after cargo ship attacked
items.summary: The ship was reportedly struck by an "unknown projectile" near Oman but no casualties were reported.

items.title: Women alleging rape and sexual assault in France call to abolish statute of limitations
items.summary: More than 50 women are calling for the abolition of the statute of limitations which they say has prevented them seeking justice.

items.title: Supreme Court allows Trump to end protected status for Haitian and Syrian immigrants
items.summary: The ruling opens the path for the Trump administration to deport hundreds of thousands of immigrants who have been living in the US for years.

items.title: Reflecting Pool liner cut with sharp knife or razor, National Park Service says
items.summary: Earlier in the week Donald Trump blamed vandals for "a 300 foot long gash" in the pool and accused someone of putting fertiliser in the water.

items.title: Asia stock markets slide as tech shares slump
items.summary: Trading on South Korea's Kospi index was halted for the third time this week to prevent panic selling.

items.title: Housing board votes to freeze New York rents in victory for Mamdani
items.summary: The decision was welcomed by many tenants - but landlords fear it will worsen housing conditions.

items.title: Row over alleged theft of donations from India's landmark Ram temple
items.summary: Questions are being raised over the handling of cash, valuable jewellery, gold and silver offered by devotees.

items.title: Paris restricts alcohol consumption and sales as Europe's heatwave shifts east
items.summary: Temperatures in Germany could hit 40C in some areas while French officials bring in alcohol restrictions to ease pressure on hospitals.

items.title: Trump asks Congress for billions for Iran war, after tension with Republicans
items.summary: But the budget faces an uphill battle as the president spars with some members of his own party over the issue.

items.title: Families lay flowers on barbed wire barricade on anniversary of deadly Kenya protests
items.summary: Protesters demand justice for the dozens killed during the 2024 demonstrations and last year's anniversary protests.

items.title: Ryanair says it will reluctantly not charge parents to sit next to children
items.summary: The airline had typically charged adults a fee of £8 each way to sit with their young children.

items.title: Oil price falls back to pre-Iran war levels
items.summary: Signs that traffic through the key Strait of Hormuz shipping route is gradually resuming has helped to push the oil price down.

items.title: Key figure in South Africa police corruption scandal pleads guilty
items.summary: Prosecutors say Vusimusi "Cat" Matlala could provide evidence against "high-ranking officials".

items.title: IBM hails new 'block of flats' design breakthrough for ultra tiny chips
items.summary: IBM says it has created the world's first known chip tech below 1 nanometre - but it will be some time before it's ready for production.

items.title: Philippines bans video game played by alleged high school shooter
items.summary: The rare school shooting that left three students killed and 20 others injured has shocked the country.

items.title: Anthropic accuses Chinese rival Alibaba of illicitly extracting AI capabilities
items.summary: The firm alleged that Alibaba used fraudulent accounts to access data from its Claude AI model.

items.title: Independent MPs launch new Australian centrist party
items.summary: Zali Steggall and Allegra Spender say Community Strong Australia will offer citizens 'reason over rage'.

items.title: Clean sweep for Mamdani-backed candidates in New York's Democratic primary
items.summary: Brad Lander unseats Dan Goldman in a race that laid bare the city's divisions over the Israel-Gaza war.

items.title: Air conditioning creates political divide after France records hottest day
items.summary: France is being forced to re-think its longstanding reservations about air-conditioning as temperatures soar.

items.title: Biggest city in Crimea without power after Ukraine strikes
items.summary: Sevastopol's Moscow-installed governor warns there will be no electricity in some areas until the evening.

items.title: UN nuclear chief says inspectors will visit Iran sites as part of war deal
items.summary: Rafael Grossi says the IAEA is "working on modalities" but an Iranian  minister says such access would only be part of a final deal with the US.

items.title: The abundant but expensive energy source that's under your feet
items.summary: Start-ups are taking fresh approaches to geothermal energy, but will the economics work?

items.title: Why the trail went cold in Nancy Guthrie case
items.summary: The lack of a breakthrough in the abduction of a famous TV anchor's mother is puzzling given the huge publicity.

items.title: A Chinese box office hit sparks a debate about identity in Singapore
items.summary: A nostalgic tale about family, hope and hardship has opened an unexpected conversation.

items.title: Could a Madison Square Garden wedding be the love story of Taylor Swift's wildest dreams?
items.summary: Officials confirmed someone applied for a permit to close the streets near Madison Square Garden on 4 July.

items.title: The ICE raids are over, but some Minnesotans say they still live in fear
items.summary: Months after the Trump administration's immigration crackdown officially ended, Minneapolis is still feeling the impact.

items.title: This Indian state is trying to ensure no one grows old alone
items.summary: Kerala is searching for new ways to care for its ageing population as migration leaves more elderly people living alone.

items.title: Aerial footage reveals destruction in coastal Venezuela
items.summary: Multi-storey buildings have collapsed in La Guaira following two major earthquakes on Wednesday.

items.title: Rescues and prayers a day after Venezuelan earthquakes
items.summary: The BBC's Vanessa Silva reports from Caracas as the city faces the aftermath of back-to-back tremors.

items.title: Watch: Moment earthquakes hit airport and buildings in Caracas
items.summary: Video filmed by BBC reporters, contributors and locals shows the aftermath, as frantic residents evacuate.

items.title: Temperatures hit record levels in western Europe
items.summary: France, Spain and Italy, have been hardest hit by the heatwave so far.
